pattern: Because every request arrives at a strictly later time than the one before it, older requests always fall out of the trailing window from the front, so a simple queue that evicts from the front as time advances is enough.
hints: Keep every request time in the order it arrived; since times are strictly increasing, the queue is always naturally sorted.
On each new ping, first add its time, then remove times from the front of the queue that are now older than the window's lower edge.
The window is inclusive on both ends: a request exactly at the lower edge still counts, and the count returned is simply how many requests remain.

edge_inputs.description: four pings with the window catching up as time advances
a single ping at the smallest allowed time
a prior request exactly at the inclusive lower edge of the window still counts
a prior request just one millisecond outside the window is excluded
several close pings followed by a large time jump that clears the window
a ping at the largest allowed time

stress.description: long strictly increasing sequence of ping times with varied gaps
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = min(max(1, size), 4000)
    ops = ['RecentCounter']
    args = [[]]
    t = 0
    for _ in range(n):
        t += rng.randint(1, 5000)
        if t > 1000000000:
            break
        ops.append('ping')
        args.append([t])
    return [ops, args]
